mainEntity.name: How much does a Mercedes-Benz SL350, SL400 or SL450 engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned M276 engine typically costs £3,000–£7,500 supply only depending on which variant (naturally aspirated or twin-turbo), with used units available from £1,800–£3,500. Fitting adds £1,500–£2,500 depending on the workshop. A 'shaft engine out repair job' on the SL350 costs about £2,000. Use our calculator for a figure specific to your SL.

mainEntity.name: Is the Mercedes-Benz SL350 / SL400 / SL450 engine reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: The M276 is generally a reliable engine, but it has well-documented weaknesses. Timing chain tensioner failure is the most common issue — causing a rattle on cold start. Carbon build-up, brittle coolant hoses and PCV system issues are also common. The naturally aspirated SL350 is generally more reliable than the twin-turbo SL400/SL450. With diligent maintenance, all can be reliable.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the Mercedes-Benz SL350, SL400 and SL450?

acceptedAnswer.text: Timing chain tensioner failure (rattling on cold start — the most critical concern), carbon build-up on intake valves (rough idle, loss of power), brittle coolant hoses (coolant leaks, overheating), PCV system failure (oil leaks, smoking exhaust), oil cooler leaks, and turbocharger issues on twin-turbo models. The timing chain tensioners are the most problematic part of the M276.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a Mercedes-Benz SL350 / SL400 / SL450 engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: SL values remain strong — a well-kept R231 is often worth £15,000–£40,000+. A £3,000–£7,500 reconditioned engine is 10–30% of the vehicle's value — apply the Mercedes 70% scrap threshold. If the car is a cherished example with good bodywork, replacement is often worthwhile. On a tired example with multiple other issues (ABC suspension, folding roof), get a scrap valuation first.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my Mercedes-Benz SL350, SL400 or SL450 engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: Listed on the VIN plate and vehicle documentation. R231 SL350 (2012–2016) uses M276 DE 35; R231 SL400 (2014–2016) uses M276 DE 30 AL; R231 SL400/SL450 (2016–2021) uses M276 DE 30 AL. Tell us your registration date and we'll confirm it for free.

mainEntity.name: Can I repair the M276 timing chain tensioner, or does the engine need replacement?

acceptedAnswer.text: The timing chain tensioners can be replaced — they are wear items. Replacement costs £800–£2,500. The most problematic part of the M276 is the timing chain tensioners. If you miss their failure, you will have to change the timing kit with phase regulators. If the chain fails and jumps timing, the engine is typically destroyed. A preventative tensioner replacement is far cheaper than a full engine replacement (£3,000+). At the first sign of rattling on cold start, book an inspection immediately.

hero.tagPill: Mercedes-Benz SL350 / SL400 / SL450 • 2012–2021 • 2 Engine Generations
hero.h1: Mercedes-Benz SL350 / SL400 / SL450 Engine Replacement — Compare Prices & Get Quotes
hero.subHeadline: The modern SL is powered by Mercedes' M276 V6 — first as a 3.5-litre naturally aspirated unit in the SL350, then as a twin-turbo 3.0-litre in the SL400 and SL450. They're smooth, refined and torquey, but timing chain rattle, carbon build-up and coolant hose brittleness are well-documented. Get quotes from UK specialists for reconditioned, rebuilt and used units today.

replacementCosts.h2: Mercedes-Benz SL350 / SL400 / SL450 Engine Replacement Costs
replacementCosts.figuresNote: Figures above reflect the current SL350/400/450 market. The naturally aspirated SL350 engine is generally more affordable to replace than the twin-turbo variants. Reconditioned engines from specialist suppliers typically start from around £3,000 supply only. A "shaft engine out repair job" on the SL350 costs about £2,000 .
replacementCosts.labourEstimate: Fitting an SL engine typically takes 12–16 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent) or £110–£160/hr (London/SE dealer). The longitudinal V6 layout makes access relatively straightforward compared to transverse layouts, but the twin-turbo setup adds complexity.

cta.label: → Compare SL350 / SL400 / SL450 engine prices from UK specialists

problems.title: Timing Chain Tensioner Failure (All M276 Variants)
problems.symptoms: Rattling or rumbling noise on cold start lasting 2–3 seconds, metallic ticking from the front of the engine, poor timing performance, engine warning light, misfires
problems.replacementCost: £3,000–£5,500 (if chain jumps and causes valve interference)

urgency.label: Immediate
urgency.text: this is the single most common issue on the M276

problems.recommendation: The M276's timing chain tensioners are the most problematic part of this engine . The issue is caused by oil draining back from the tensioners when the engine is parked, causing a rattle for a few seconds until oil pressure returns . If you miss their failure, you will have to change the timing kit with phase regulators . If you hear rattling on cold start, book an inspection immediately. A preventative tensioner replacement is far cheaper than a full engine replacement.

failureLink.label: Read the full timing chain failure guide

problems.title: Carbon Build-up on Intake Valves (All M276 Variants)
problems.symptoms: Rough idle, reduced performance, poor fuel economy, hesitation on acceleration
problems.recommendation: Like all direct-injection engines, the M276 suffers from carbon build-up on the intake valves . Regular cleaning and the use of specific additives can help prevent long-term damage. If you notice loss of power or rough idle, have the intake valves inspected. Walnut blasting is the standard cleaning method and is well-understood by Mercedes specialists.

failureLink.label: Read the full carbon build-up guide

problems.title: Brittle Coolant Hoses and Cooling System Leaks (All M276 Variants)
problems.symptoms: Coolant loss, overheating, temperature spikes, visible drips near the engine
problems.recommendation: The M276's coolant hoses can become brittle from heat exposure, cracking over time and leading to coolant loss and potential overheating . Regular inspection and timely replacement of hoses is essential . If you notice coolant loss or temperature spikes, have the cooling system inspected immediately. A £300–£800 hose replacement is far cheaper than the consequences of an overheated engine.

failureLink.label: Read the full coolant leak guide

problems.title: Turbocharger Issues (Twin-Turbo M276 — SL400/SL450)
problems.symptoms: Loss of power, excessive smoke, high-pitched noises from the engine bay, poor acceleration
problems.recommendation: The twin-turbo M276 can suffer from turbocharger issues caused by oil leaks, worn bearings, or clogged oil supply lines . If you notice loss of power, excessive smoke, or high-pitched noises, have the turbochargers inspected immediately. Regular oil changes and maintaining the oil supply lines are essential to prevent turbo failure .

failureLink.label: Read the full turbocharger failure guide

repairBuyOrReplace.h2: SL350 / SL400 / SL450 — Repair, Buy, or Replace?

rows.whenItMakesSense: Labour-intensive but far cheaper than a full engine replacement
If chain jumps and valves hit pistons, replacement is the only option
Walnut blasting — well-understood by Mercedes specialists
Catch before debris damages the engine
Replacement — prevent oil leaks and smoking exhaust

buyingChecks.title: Buying This SL350 / SL400 / SL450? Check These First
buyIf: * Naturally aspirated SL350 (2012–2016) — generally more reliable than the twin-turbo variants
* Evidence of timing chain tensioner replacement — the single most important check on the M276
* Full service history with regular oil changes (every 10,000 miles) — the M276 rewards good maintenance
* Evidence of carbon cleaning and cooling system maintenance
* Post-2016 facelift with documented turbocharger service (SL400/SL450)
avoidIf: * Any rattling on cold start — timing chain tensioner wear is likely
* SL400/SL450 with turbocharger issues or loss of power
* Evidence of repeated coolant top-ups without a diagnosed leak — cooling system issues
* Poor service history — the M276 is unforgiving of neglect

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The modern SL is powered by the M276 V6 family — a refined and capable engine that comes in naturally aspirated and twin-turbo forms. The naturally aspirated SL350 (2012–2016) is generally more reliable than the twin-turbo variants. All M276 engines share common weaknesses: timing chain tensioner failure (cold-start rattle), carbon build-up, brittle coolant hoses, and PCV system issues. The twin-turbo SL400 and SL450 add turbocharger complexity to the mix. Buy on history, maintain on schedule, and the SL will reward you with one of the most refined grand touring experiences available.

cta.label: → Compare SL350 / SL400 / SL450 engine prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used Mercedes-Benz SL350, SL400 or SL450 (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: 1. Cold Start — Listen for rattling lasting 2–3 seconds (timing chain tensioner warning — the most critical check on the M276)
2. Service History — including which engine (naturally aspirated or twin-turbo) and evidence of major maintenance
3. Carbon Build-up — Any rough idle or loss of power? (intake valve cleaning needed)
4. Coolant Level — Any unexplained loss or overheating? (coolant hose or thermostat housing warning)
5. Turbo Health (SL400/SL450) — Any loss of power, smoke or high-pitched noises? (turbocharger issues)
6. Oil Leaks — Any oil under the car or around the engine? (oil cooler seals or PCV system)
7. Engine Warning Light — Have codes been read? Any unresolved issues?
8. MOT History — Check for emissions-related failures (catalyst, O2 sensors)
